title: বাংলাদেশ টেলিযোগাযোগ আইন, ২০০১
description: The Bangladesh Telecommunication Act 2001 establishes the regulatory framework for telecommunications services and networks in Bangladesh, creating the Bangladesh Telecommunication Regulatory Commission (BTRC) as the independent regulator. The Act governs the licensing of telecommunications service providers, spectrum management, interconnection, consumer protection, and universal service obligations.

provisions.title: Section 1. Short Title and Commencement
provisions.content: This Act may be called the Bangladesh Telecommunication Act, 2001. It extends to the whole of Bangladesh. It comes into force on such date as the Government may, by notification in the official Gazette, appoint. The Act establishes the comprehensive regulatory framework for telecommunications in Bangladesh, creating the Bangladesh Telecommunication Regulatory Commission as an independent statutory body with powers to regulate, supervise, and enforce applicable requirements across all categories of telecommunications service providers. The Act aims to promote the development of telecommunications infrastructure, ensure quality service delivery, protect consumer interests, and advance universal access to telecommunications services.

provisions.title: Section 2. Definitions
provisions.content: In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires: 'Telecommunication' means any transmission, emission, or reception of signs, signals, writing, images, sounds, or intelligence of any nature by wire, radio, optical, or other electromagnetic systems; 'Telecommunication service' means any service of transmitting, emitting, or receiving signs, signals, writing, images, sounds, or intelligence of any nature by telecommunications networks; 'Bangladesh Telecommunication Regulatory Commission' or 'BTRC' means the Commission established under this Act; 'Licence' means a licence granted under this Act for the purpose of establishing or operating a telecommunications system or providing a telecommunications service; 'Spectrum' means the radio frequency spectrum; 'Universal service' means a basic standard of telecommunications service that is available to all citizens of Bangladesh at an affordable price; 'Significant market power' means a position in a relevant market that enables an operator to behave independently of competitors and consumers.

provisions.title: Section 3. Bangladesh Telecommunication Regulatory Commission
provisions.content: There shall be established a Commission to be called the Bangladesh Telecommunication Regulatory Commission. The Commission shall be a body corporate with perpetual succession and common seal, capable of suing and being sued, and able to acquire, hold and dispose of property. The Commission shall consist of a Chairman and four Commissioners, all appointed by the Government. The Commission shall be independent in the exercise of its regulatory functions and shall not be subject to the direction or control of any person or authority in the exercise of its powers and functions under this Act. The Commission shall: grant, modify, suspend, and revoke licences; manage the radio frequency spectrum; regulate tariffs; resolve disputes; protect consumer interests; and promote the development of telecommunications.

provisions.title: Section 4. Licences
provisions.content: No person shall establish or operate a telecommunications system or provide a telecommunications service without a licence granted by the BTRC under this Act. Licences are granted for specific categories of service including: public switched telephone networks; mobile cellular networks; internet services; international gateway services; submarine cable systems; and other specified categories. The BTRC shall determine the number of licences to be granted in each category, the applicable licence conditions, fees, and the procedure for granting licences. Licences shall specify the services that may be provided, the geographic areas covered, applicable quality of service requirements, and coverage obligations. Licence conditions shall not be modified without prior notice to and consultation with the licence holder. Licences are not transferable without the prior approval of the BTRC.

provisions.title: Section 5. Spectrum Management
provisions.content: The radio frequency spectrum is a national resource under the ownership and administration of the State. The BTRC shall manage the radio frequency spectrum in accordance with applicable national and international regulations, including the ITU Radio Regulations. The BTRC shall develop and maintain a National Frequency Allocation Plan consistent with ITU Radio Regulations and aligned with regional spectrum coordination frameworks. Rights to use specific radio frequency bands are assigned by the BTRC through licensing processes, which may include competitive mechanisms where demand exceeds available spectrum. Spectrum licences are subject to conditions including coverage requirements, technical parameters, and payment of spectrum fees. The BTRC shall monitor spectrum use and shall take action against unlicensed use and harmful interference.

provisions.title: Section 6. Interconnection
provisions.content: Licensed telecommunications operators are obliged to provide interconnection with other licensed operators at their request, on non-discriminatory terms and conditions. The BTRC shall establish a Reference Interconnection Offer (RIO) framework requiring operators with significant market power to publish reference offers specifying interconnection prices, terms, and conditions. Interconnection pricing shall be cost-oriented for operators with significant market power and shall be approved by the BTRC. Interconnection agreements shall be filed with the BTRC within thirty days of their conclusion. The BTRC shall resolve interconnection disputes where commercial negotiations fail. The BTRC may mandate specific interconnection terms for operators with significant market power, including access to local loops and co-location facilities.

provisions.title: Section 7. Universal Service
provisions.content: The Government shall pursue universal service objectives to ensure that basic telecommunications services are available to all citizens of Bangladesh, including in rural and remote areas. The BTRC shall administer a Universal Service Obligation (USO) fund financed by contributions from licensed operators. Contributions to the USO fund shall be set as a percentage of operators' adjusted gross revenues, as determined by the BTRC. The BTRC shall use the USO fund to subsidise the deployment of telecommunications infrastructure and services in areas where commercial provision is not viable. Universal service programmes shall prioritise connectivity for rural communities, educational institutions, health facilities, and local government. The BTRC shall report annually on the state of universal service in Bangladesh and on the use of USO fund resources.

provisions.title: Section 8. Consumer Protection
provisions.content: Licensed telecommunications operators shall treat their customers fairly, provide clear and accurate information about services and pricing, and maintain effective complaint handling mechanisms. The BTRC shall establish quality of service standards for telecommunications services and shall monitor compliance by licensed operators. The BTRC shall operate a consumer complaint mechanism enabling users of telecommunications services to file complaints about service quality, billing disputes, and other issues. Licensed operators shall respond to consumer complaints within specified timeframes and shall resolve them in a transparent and fair manner. Consumers shall have the right to number portability when changing service provider, subject to applicable regulations. The BTRC shall publish comparative information about service quality and consumer complaints enabling consumers to make informed choices.

provisions.title: Section 9. Tariff Regulation
provisions.content: The BTRC shall regulate the tariffs charged by licensed telecommunications operators, particularly those with significant market power. Tariff regulation shall be based on principles of cost orientation, transparency, and non-discrimination. Operators with significant market power shall file their tariffs with the BTRC and shall not charge tariffs that have not been approved by or notified to the BTRC. The BTRC may order operators to reduce tariffs that are found to be excessive or that distort competition. The BTRC shall ensure that tariffs for universal service obligations are affordable, taking into account the socioeconomic characteristics of different segments of the population. Retail tariffs for competitive markets shall be freely set by operators, subject to applicable competition law and the BTRC's monitoring of anti-competitive practices.

provisions.title: Section 10. Network Security Obligations
provisions.content: Licensed telecommunications operators shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure the security and integrity of their networks. Security measures shall address: protection against unauthorised access to network elements; protection against malware and other cyber threats; availability and resilience of network infrastructure; and protection of customer data and communications. Operators shall maintain incident detection and response capabilities and shall report significant security incidents to the BTRC and to the national CIRT within specified timeframes. Operators shall cooperate with national cybersecurity authorities in security investigations and shall implement security improvements recommended by the BTRC or the DSA. The BTRC shall issue technical guidelines for network security applicable to licensed operators.

provisions.title: Section 11. Lawful Interception
provisions.content: Licensed telecommunications operators shall maintain technical capability for the lawful interception of communications by authorised government agencies in accordance with applicable law. Lawful interception requests shall be made only by designated government authorities pursuant to lawful authority including applicable judicial or executive authorisation. Operators shall comply with lawful interception requests promptly and shall maintain the confidentiality of such requests. The technical standards and procedures for lawful interception shall be established by the BTRC in coordination with relevant government security agencies. Operators shall ensure that their lawful interception capabilities are not accessible to unauthorised parties. Compliance with lawful interception obligations shall be monitored by the BTRC through periodic audits.

provisions.title: Section 12. Significant Market Power
provisions.content: The BTRC shall conduct periodic market analyses to identify operators with significant market power in relevant telecommunications markets. An operator shall be deemed to have significant market power where it has the ability to act to an appreciable extent independently of competitors and consumers in a relevant market. Where the BTRC determines that one or more operators have significant market power, it may impose obligations including: transparency; non-discrimination; access to specific facilities; interconnection at cost-oriented prices; and accounting separation. Obligations imposed on operators with significant market power shall be proportionate to the competition problem identified and shall be reviewed at regular intervals. The BTRC shall publish its significant market power determinations and the associated obligations.

provisions.title: Section 13. Sanctions and Enforcement
provisions.content: The BTRC shall have the authority to impose sanctions on licensed operators that fail to comply with the provisions of this Act, applicable regulations, or their licence conditions. Sanctions may include: warnings; financial penalties; suspension of licences; and revocation of licences. Financial penalties shall be calibrated to the gravity of the violation, the harm caused, and the economic benefit derived by the operator from non-compliance. In determining sanctions, the BTRC shall take into account: the nature and duration of the violation; the degree of culpability; any remedial action taken by the operator; and the operator's compliance history. An operator subject to a sanction may appeal to the competent administrative tribunal within thirty days of the sanction.

provisions.title: Section 14. International Relations
provisions.content: The Government of Bangladesh shall fulfil its obligations under applicable international telecommunications agreements, including the ITU Constitution and Convention, and shall participate actively in ITU bodies. The BTRC shall represent Bangladesh in international telecommunications regulatory forums and shall coordinate with regional telecommunications regulatory bodies. Spectrum allocations shall be coordinated with neighbouring countries and with ITU in accordance with applicable Radio Regulations. The Government shall promote regional telecommunications integration within applicable frameworks and shall work towards harmonised regulatory approaches with regional partners. International submarine cable systems landing in Bangladesh shall be subject to specific licensing and security requirements established by the BTRC.

provisions.title: Section 15. Broadband Development
provisions.content: The BTRC shall develop and maintain a National Broadband Policy aimed at achieving universal access to affordable high-speed internet services. The National Broadband Policy shall establish: coverage targets for broadband availability across Bangladesh; speed and quality standards for broadband services; mechanisms for financing broadband deployment in underserved areas; and indicators for measuring broadband development. The BTRC shall publish an annual Broadband Status Report tracking progress against national targets. The Government shall support broadband infrastructure deployment in underserved areas through the USO fund and other applicable mechanisms. The BTRC shall facilitate the efficient deployment of broadband infrastructure through streamlined permitting processes and infrastructure sharing requirements.

definitions.definition: Bangladesh Telecommunication Regulatory Commission, the independent regulatory body for telecommunications.
Any transmission, emission, or reception of signs, signals, writing, images, sounds, or intelligence by wire, radio, optical, or other electromagnetic systems.
The radio frequency spectrum, a national resource administered by the BTRC.
A basic standard of telecommunications service available to all citizens at an affordable price.
A position enabling an operator to act independently of competitors and consumers in a relevant market.
The physical and logical linking of telecommunications networks enabling users of different networks to communicate.
